# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF MENTAL HEALTH: The Cure For Overthinking*

- id: 41878133
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/41878133

---

##    🔑 8 TRIFECTA III: The Art Of Stopping 

High-level creativity comes from deep focus followed by deep rest.   


If you've been feeling daytime energy crashes, insomnia, or you always wake up multiple times a night-- you probably have bad sleep hygiene.

When you're serious about sleep, you set TWO alarms. One for when you want to wake up in the morning. And the other (more important) one for when it's time to start your "wind-down" ritual. Without a wind-down ritual, you're ripe for insomnia and interrupted sleep patterns.

The goal of “wind down" is to LOWER your heartbeat in preparation for the most recuperative sleep possible. (Ideas below) Any activity that RAISES your heart beat, isn’t it.   


Sleep hygiene = healthy habits BEFORE sleep.  


If you want to make more art, generate more flow moments, or even party harder without needing drugs… Start getting obsessive about your sleep hygiene.  


Like most good things, the results won't be instant. But if you hang in there, your life will change for the incredible within a few weeks.

  


#  **Part I: Here are 10 'sleep hygiene rules I live by when I'm entering a new phase of outrageous creative output:**  


1\. Go to bed at the same time every night. It worked for Picasso (3 am). It works for Steven King (10 pm).

2\. Never sleep in. But remember, "sleeping in" is relative. It isn't about what time you wake up. This rule only means don't sleep beyond 8 or 9 hours. If you don't feel rested after 8 hours, take a nap later instead.

3\. Naps are magical. At the beginning... You may have to pay the price of insomnia with some non-restful nights. Compensate by napping early in the day (only once). Do it sometime between 11am - 2pm. And for 20-30 minutes max. That's how you'll still be able to fall asleep easier that night. Use this until your insomnia goes away and your circadian clock is re-set for your new bedtime.

4\. Have your last meal as far away from "lights out" as possible. And avoid spicy food.

5\. Don't drink alcohol to "help you sleep". Yeah, you may doze off faster, but it prohibits DEEP SLEEP. Why should you care? 'Deep sleep' is the sleeping stage for body and brain repair. This is when your immune system gets strengthened. If you want to be one of those people who only gets sick once every couple of years, here's the key. 

6\. Create a "wind down ritual" for yourself. (THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT RULE) What you do one hour before “lights off" is EVERYTHING. Set an alarm for when it begins so you STOP WORKING. Shut off screens (yes that means phones, laptops, IPADs, AND also TV). Dim the lights. Now you're ready for wind down (more below).

7\. Sleep in a slightly cooled room. Lower temperatures are a signal for the body to fall asleep. It also helps you STAY asleep as your body temperature lowers at night.

8\. Only use your bed for sleeping (and f-cking). Don't eat in bed. Don't work in bed. Reading in bed is fine if it's during your wind-down ritual.

9\. Exercise during the day. Fit bodies sleep way easier. 

10\. (ONLY FOR THE HARDCORE) Go outside in your first 20 minutes of awake time. The morning sun's light energy (even on a cloudy day) resets your melatonin to fire off at the right time later that night to get you "sleepy" for bed.

  


# **Part II: Wind Down Ideas**

1\. Burn scented oils, incense or candles and bask in the dim light doing nothing else.

2\. Read a book. Pick one that has nothing to do with your life (no self help). A book that will transport you into someone else’s life (biographies are excellent, and so are fantasy or mystery novels).

3\. Meditation or NSDR (Non-sleep, Deep Rest) for 30 min. Then just lie there.

4\. Warm Shower Or Bath.

5\. Cleaning your space/room/house in complete silence like the monks do.

6\. Listening to calming music (a playlist or album you already know and love).

7\. A meditative journaling brain dump.

8\. Gratitude session out loud, while smiling ear to ear.

9\. Gentle Stretches. Stretching properly starts by creating a little tension… And then teaching your body to relax during that tension… You do this through controlled, slow “nose to mouth” breathing. (Nose “in”… Mouth “out…) Hold your stretches for 3 to 5 minutes. Anything less isn't enough for your body parts to "learn" what you're teaching it. But the best side effect of night stretches is that all of that relaxing breathing makes you hella sleepy. And it quietens an overthinking mind. 

10\. Nothing. The most radical and replenishing of them all. It’s a law of physics that “nature abhors a vacuum”. So when you give your mind that space, what comes back in is… you. Your essence. 

  


# **Part III: The Fast Sleep Military Protocol (For Creatives)**

## 1\. Read for 5 to 10 minutes. This moves your mind away from your regular thoughts.   


## 2\. Lights off.  


## 3\. Lay on your side to start. (You can switch to your fave position later).  


## 4\. Breathe deeply. In through nose quickly (filling up stomach). Sneak in a sniffle more, then… Hold for a count of 7. Exhale slowly out through the mouth. Do this 4 times. That’s all you need.  


## 5\. Relax your eyes. (It could help to gently put your fingers on your eyelids first so you “feel” them. And to make sure they are relaxed.)  


## 6\. Relax your face. Try to feel if there's any tension on your face. Mouth. Forehead. Ears. And relax those areas completely.  


## 7\. Visualize yourself doing something you love. I usually visualize creating a synth patch from scratch.  


You should be in lala land by now… You may be sleep anxious (when you don’t sleep because you’re worried about not sleeping), after having battled insomnia for a while. If so, just start again from STEP 4, until your mind gives in.   


Will everything be perfect every night? Of course not. "Life" is not a straight line. It will throw you curve balls. Don't let discipline be your failing. That lame. Only real-life events like weddings, birthdays, or consoling a friend should disrupt your routine… And when it does (it always does)... It's all good. Just slip back into your creativity-inducing rituals the following day.  


I'm excited for you to figure out your own "wind down ritual" and perfect your own version of the "fast sleep protocol". Let me know if you come up with amazing personal practices that I can share.  
  


# **PART IV. Insomnia (the "wake-up, but then can’t fall asleep" kind)**

This is how you train your body to fall asleep quickly after you wake up in the middle of the night. This could take as long as 2 weeks to fix. But you have to do ALL the steps for it to work. Frustration won’t help you at all.  


## 1\. No late-night TV or screens. Read instead.  


## 2\. Don’t check your clock at all when you wake up. All it will do is stress you out. Sunrise will tell you when you need to get up.  


## 3\. Stay in one position (pillow between knees helps this)  


## 4\. Breathe only through the nose (with long, slow exhales)  


## 5\. And relax about the fact that you can’t sleep. Accept it, and surrender to it. It’ll be fixed by next week if you follow this to the letter.  


[ Complete and Continue   ]()

Imma pile on this post and also add in making YOGA NIDRA the last thing you do for the day. It uses your brain's natural wiring to put your asleep (a full body scan done in a particular order). It has been knocking me out every night for the last 3 years. You can also do it during the day instead of a nap (Andrew Huberman also sings its praises as NSDR - Non Sleep Deep Rest) 🙌🏼

When I was a child, my mother came to my bed to pray a "good night prayer" with me. Nowadays I do not believe in Chistian dogma, but I found out that I can still list all the people I care about in my mind and thank for the things I am thankful for just before closing my eyes. <3

a year ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/41878133/comments/25603494)

Wow! My brother once told me the fast sleep military protocol like ten years ago. I am so impressed to see it here too! I don't even know where he read about it but it works like a charm. On some stressful days I have to repeat breathing-relaxing-imagining cycle for a few times before I doze out but it still works. The worst advice I heard somewhere on the Internet - if you can't sleep, then don't waste your time, go do some work until you are more tired. THIS IS THE WORST ADVICE. Your next day will be destroyed utterly and you WILL waste the WHOLE day instead of 15 minutes of relaxation. And more... I believe it will give long-term consequences to your brain. If you want to know which, read "The Black Monk" story written by Anton Chekhov.

This is fun, I have been lighting a little bit of palo santo right before bed and have created a ritual where when I smell it im immediately taken into a very relaxed state. It took a bit but i have finally found a rhythm and ended the insomnia.

So obvious, yet I never had an evening wind down ritual. I am quite good with the morning one, but realized I have this mindset: I do the morning ritual and then let life overtake as I can't control the whole day as much as a section of time in the morning. Evening ritual. That's the new "fast lane" for me, I have a feeling.

SaraMarie Bottaro

Everything seems to be aligning about sleep for me lately! I have a bad habit of being busy all the way until I need it to be lights out. I've been making progress in fits and starts with it but now is the time to really commit. Thank you for being intense about health in all ways

Thankyou! Another incredible tool that helped my insomnia was the shakti mat. An acupressure mat. I use it for 20-30 minutes lying on it in bed or on the ground and I go into the deepest relaxation place, which then sets me up for a deep sleep
